title: Ulundhu Vithakkaiyile Lyrics Mudhalvan
stanzas:
ulunthu vedhaikaiyile
suthi oodha kaathu adikaiyile
naan appanukku kanji kondu
aathu maedu thaandi ponen
kanda nalla nalla sagunathil
nenjukuli poothu ponen.(2)

rekkapadappil gawuli kaththa
valibu pakkam garudan suththa
theruoram nera kudam paarkavum
mani saththam kaetkavum
aanathe.

oru pookkari edhuka vara
pasum paal maadu kadakkirathe
ini ennaagume yedhagumo
indha sirikki vazhiyil
deivam pugundhu
varam tharumo...

anichcham malar azhage
achu achuvellam pechazhage
en kannukulla koodu katti
kaadhukulla koovum kuyile.
nee etti etti pogaiyile
vittu vittu pogum uyire.(2)

oru thadava iluthu anaichapadi
uyir moocha niruthu kanmaniye
ummudhuga tholaichu veliyera
innum konja irukku ennavane

mazhai adikkum siru pechu
veyil adikkum siru paarvai.
odambu mannu pudhaigira
varaiyil udan vara koodumo.

usur ennodu irukkaiyile
nee mannodu povadhenge
ada un jeevane
inge naanillaiyaa
kolla vandha maranam kooda
kuzhambumaiyaa ..

kurukku siruththavale
enna kungumathil karaichavale
manja thaechi kulikaiyil
enna konjam poosithaaye
kolusukkul maniyaaga
enna konjam maathu thaaye.

oru kannil neer kasiya
udhatu vazhi usur kasiya
unnaala sila murai irakkavum
sila murai pirakkavum aanathe

ada aathoda vilundha ilai
andha aathoda povadhu pol
nenju unnoduthaan pinnoduthe
ada kaalam marandhu
kaattu maramum pookkirathe.

kurukku siruththavale
enna kungumathil karaichavale
manja thaechi kulikaiyil
unna konjam poosuvenyaa
kolusukkul maniyaaga
enna konjam maathuvenyaa.

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song Ulundhu Vithakkaiyile?

The song conveys themes of joy and celebration, reflecting the happiness in life's simple moments.

How does this song fit into the plot of Mudhalvan?

In Mudhalvan, the song highlights the protagonist's journey and the joyous moments he experiences along the way.

Who are the singers of this song?

The song features the talented voices of Srinivas and Swarnalatha.

Who composed the music for Ulundhu Vithakkaiyile?

The music was composed by the renowned AR Rahman.

When was Ulundhu Vithakkaiyile released?

The song was released on November 7, 1999.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In Ulundhu Vithakkaiyile, the vibrant energy of the song encapsulates the celebratory spirit of life, making it a perfect fit for joyous occasions. The lyrics reflect a sense of nostalgia and happiness, resonating with listeners as it highlights the beauty of simple moments. Sung by the talented Srinivas and Swarnalatha, this duet showcases AR Rahman's exceptional musical composition. The song evokes feelings of joy and celebration, making it a timeless favorite in Tamil cinema.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Ulundhu Vithakkaiyile is a vibrant duet that captures the essence of celebration and joy, showcasing the unique collaboration between the soulful voices of Srinivas and Swarnalatha. With AR Rahman's masterful composition, this song from the film Mudhalvan resonates with an infectious energy that uplifts the spirit. Dive into this melodious journey that perfectly encapsulates the festive vibe of the movie!
stanzaMeanings: In the field of grains, I went around, bringing porridge for my father, crossing over to the good side, and blooming in my heart with good feelings.
In the sky, the eagle flies, and I can see the bright streets. I can hear the sound of the bell and see the water pot.
A flower picker comes to take the green milk cow, but what will happen now? Will God come and bless me on this smiling path?
The beautiful flower of desire has bloomed, and everything is talking. In my eyes, the nest is built, and the cuckoo sings in my heart. You are leaving slowly, my life is slipping away.
Once again, I write, stopping my breath. My dear, you have left me, and there is still some time left for me.
The rain brings small talks, and the sun brings small glances. Will the body come together until the earth is renewed?
My life is with you, why are you leaving? Even death that came here is childish...
Oh little one, why are you scratching my saffron? In the yellow water, why are you pouring a little? In the pot, why are you changing a little?
In one eye, there is water, and in the other, there is a way to salvation. Because of you, sometimes I feel lost and sometimes I feel born.
Oh, the fallen leaf, like that, the heart is behind you. The time has forgotten, and the tree is blooming.
Oh little one, why are you scratching my saffron? In the yellow water, I will pour a little. In the pot, I will change a little.
